summary: Average caseloads for ACS child protective specialists have dropped from a recent high of 9.7 cases in Fiscal 2023 to roughly 6 by Fiscal 2026. ACS's own performance reports attribute this to a deliberate staffing strategy: hiring more caseworkers ahead of expected attrition and keeping more case-assignable staff available at any given time, rather than a drop in the number of cases needing attention.

sources.label: ACS, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report
